1. **Duplication across all projects:**
   - `TargetFramework` net8.0 - repeated in all 5 projects

, though MSBuild handles the deduplication

# MSBuild Project File Review — ContosoApp Solution

## 1. Prioritized Issues

### 🔴 Errors (Likely to Cause Problems)

| # | Issue | Location | Impact |
|---|-------|----------|--------|
| 1 | **Package version drift — `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions`** | Core/Api: `8.0.0`, Services: `8.0.1` | Can cause assembly binding conflicts at runtime (MSB3277). The diamond dependency (Api → Core `8.0.0` + Api → Services `8.0.1`) forces NuGet to pick one, which may surprise developers. |
| 2 | **Test framework version drift** | Core.Tests: `xunit 2.8.1`, `runner 2.8.1`, `Test.Sdk 17.10.0`; Api.Tests: `xunit 2.7.1`, `runner 2.5.6`, `Test.Sdk 17.9.0` | Tests may behave differently across projects. A bug fixed in 2.8.1 may be missed in Api.Tests. Makes debugging test infrastructure issues much harder. |

### 🟡 Warnings (Anti-Patterns to Fix)

| # | Issue | Location | Impact |
|---|-------|----------|--------|
| 3 | **Massive property duplication across all 5 projects** | All `.csproj` files | 7 properties (`TargetFramework`, `Nullable`, `ImplicitUsings`, `TreatWarningsAsErrors`, `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild`, `Company`, `Authors`) copy-pasted in every project. Changing one setting requires editing 5 files. |
| 4 | **No Central Package Management** | All projects | 10+ package versions are scattered across 5 files. Version drift (issues #1 and #2) is the direct consequence. |
| 5 | **Duplicated `PrintBuildInfo` target** | Core, Api, Services | Identical target in 3 files. Should be in `Directory.Build.targets`. |
| 6 | **`PrintBuildInfo` target lacks `Inputs`/`Outputs`** | Core, Api, Services | Runs on every single build unconditionally. While it's just a `Message` task (lightweight), this sets a bad precedent — custom targets without I/O break incremental builds. |
| 7 | **Redundant `System.Text.Json` in Api** | Api `.csproj` | Api already gets `System.Text.Json 8.0.5` transitively via `ContosoApp.Core`. The explicit reference creates a maintenance burden (must keep versions in sync manually). |
| 8 | **`xunit.runner.visualstudio` missing `PrivateAssets="all"`** | Both test projects | This is a test infrastructure package that should never flow to consuming projects. Without `PrivateAssets="all"`, it could appear in dependency graphs if the test project is ever referenced. |
| 9 | **Dual JSON serialization libraries** | Core: `System.Text.Json`, Services: `Newtonsoft.Json` | The Api project transitively depends on both. This increases package surface, binary size, and forces developers to know two APIs. Consider standardizing (unless `Newtonsoft.Json` is needed for a specific scenario). |

### 🔵 Suggestions (Improvements)

| # | Issue | Location | Impact |
|---|-------|----------|--------|
| 10 | **`DocumentationFile` duplicated in 3 src projects** | Core, Api, Services | Can be centralized. Also, with modern .NET, you can use `<GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>` which is cleaner (the SDK infers the path). |
| 11 | **Multiple `<PropertyGroup>` blocks in same file** | Core, Api, Services | The `DocumentationFile` is in a separate `<PropertyGroup>` for no apparent reason. Merge into the first one for readability. |
| 12 | **Missing `coverlet.collector`** | Both test projects | No code coverage collector is configured. Consider adding it for CI pipelines. |
| 13 | **Test projects missing `<IsTestProject>true</IsTestProject>`** | Both test projects | This is auto-inferred by the Test SDK, but being explicit improves discoverability and tooling. |

### 🟢 Positive (Done Well)

- ✅ All projects use SDK-style format — no legacy projects to migrate
- ✅ `TreatWarningsAsErrors` is enabled everywhere — good code quality hygiene
- ✅ `Nullable` enabled — embracing null-safety
- ✅ `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` enabled — consistent styling
- ✅ Test projects correctly set `IsPackable` to `false`
- ✅ Clean project references — no circular dependencies

---

## 2. Recommended Centralized Files

### `Directory.Build.props` (at repo root)

```xml
<Project>
  <!--
    Shared properties for all projects in the solution.
    See: https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/project-sdk/msbuild-props
  -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Company/authorship metadata (for NuGet packages) -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <PropertyGroup Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Test project defaults -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
    <IsTestProject>true</IsTestProject>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Central Package Management -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

### `Directory.Build.targets` (at repo root)
